MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-13-2006 Assigned To Trial Court

Notice Of Renewed Motion For Order To Augment Expert Witness List
Off Calendar Feb-23-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Bert Carter, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
Cassandra Knight, appeared for Defendant/opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Renewed Motion For Order To Augment Expert Witness List is denied. The court does not find any new facts, circumstances, or law which support Plaintiff's motion. As to the alleged new facts, a party must show that the alleged new facts were not reasonably available at the time of the previous hearing. There is nothing that would support such a finding.
Neither is there any new law which would lead to a different ruling. The Solv-All v Superior Court, 131 Cal.App.4th 1003, incorrectly cited in the moving papers, supports an interpretation of excusable neglect in the context of a default, but not in the context of a discovery ruling. There is no attorney fault provision in the applicable sections, C.C.P. 2033.300 and C.C.P. 2033.620. There is no authority for the argument that C.C.P. Section 473 applies to discovery proceedings.
The court finds that the motion was brought without substantial justification. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff and his counsel in the amount of $340, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn For Order To Augment Expert Witness List & Declaration & For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-19-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Bert Carter Jr, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party Nathaniel Jackson
Cassandra Knight, Appeared for Defendant/Opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Brejon Jackson's Motion To Augment Expert Witness List is Denied. The court does not find that Plaintiff failed to submit the expert disclosure as a result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. The court finds that Plaintiff failed to make the disclosure as a strategic choice and seeks to make the late disclosure long after discovery closed. All requests for sanctions are denied.COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
